url.loc:
https://blendedmockdewittsolutions.netlify.Since you don't have a sitemap yet, we can create one based on the routes defined in your `App.jsx`. A sitemap acts as a roadmap for search engines, ensuring they find and index all your important pages like the Menu and About sections.
### 1. Create your `sitemap.xml` Create a file named `sitemap.xml` in your **`public`** folder and paste the following content. I have included your main customer-facing routes and excluded internal ones like `/checkout` or `/order/:id` (which shouldn't be indexed). ```xml
<urlset xmlns="http://www.s</itemaps.org/schemas/sitemap/0.9">
[https://blendedmockdewittsolutions.netlify.app/](https://blendedmockdewittsolutions.netlify.app/) 2026-05-06 weekly 1.0
https://blendedmock```
### 2. Strategic Exclusions You’ll notice I excluded `/checkout`, `/orderdewittsolutions.netlify.app/about 2026-05-06 monthly 0.8
https://blendedmockdewittsolutions.netlify.app/menu 2026-05-06 /return`, and `/order/:orderId`. * **Checkout:** You don't want Google to index a half-finished transaction page. * **Ordersweekly 0.9
:** Since order IDs are dynamic and unique to customers, they shouldn't be publicly indexed in search results.
---
### Pro-Tips for Your Reacthttps://blendedmockdewittsolutions.netlify.app/contact 2026-05-06 monthly 0.7
</urlset Routes
**1. Managing SEO per Route**
